I was not editing DEX there; those `R ... -> ...` lines are Git rename records already present in your working tree.

Why this matters: they look like active DEX work, but they are repo-state output, not bridge commands I executed.

Facts (confirmed):
- `R` means Git detected a rename/move from old path to new path.
- All lines you pasted are DEX path renames (`pancake-swap-v1-bsc-testnet` -> `bullswap-v1-gilt-testnet`).
- My direct edits in this step were bridge docs only, not DEX code.

Inference (likely cause):
- The repository is very dirty, so unrelated DEX rename entries showed up while we were working bridge scope.

Actions (recommended next steps):
1. Ignore these DEX `R` lines for bridge scope.
2. If you want, we can do a separate DEX-only review later; otherwise I stay bridge-only.

Completion level: complete.
